sections.title: Types of number
sections.core_content: Identify and use: natural numbers, integers, primes, squares, cubes, triangular numbers, rational and irrational numbers, real numbers, reciprocals. Identify common factors, common multiples, HCF and LCM. Write a number as a product of its prime factors.
sections.extended_content: Also use relevant notation and vocabulary for sets of numbers.

sections.title: Sets and Venn diagrams
sections.core_content: Use set language, notation and Venn diagrams to describe sets and represent relationships between sets. Definition of sets: e.g. A = {x : x is a natural number}, B = {(x, y) : y = mx + c}. Notation: n(A), ∈, ∉, complement, empty set, universal set, A ⊂ B, A ⊄ B, union, intersection.
sections.extended_content: Use Venn diagrams with up to three sets to solve problems.

sections.title: Powers and roots
sections.core_content: Calculate with squares, square roots, cubes, cube roots and other powers and roots of numbers. Use index notation and index laws for multiplication and division of positive integer powers.
sections.extended_content: Use and interpret fractional indices (e.g. a^(1/2) = √a) and negative indices (e.g. a^(-n) = 1/a^n). Apply the rules of indices.

sections.title: Directed numbers
sections.core_content: Use directed numbers in practical situations. Order directed numbers. Apply the four operations to directed numbers.

sections.title: Fractions, decimals and percentages
sections.core_content: Use the language and notation of fractions, decimals and percentages including proper fractions, improper fractions, mixed numbers, decimals, percentages. Recognise equivalences and convert between these forms. Order fractions and decimals.
sections.extended_content: Convert recurring decimals to fractions and vice versa.

sections.title: Order of operations
sections.core_content: Use the standard order of operations (BIDMAS/BODMAS) including correct use of brackets. Understand that multiplication and division have equal priority and are performed left to right, as do addition and subtraction.

sections.title: Bounds and rounding
sections.core_content: Round values to a specified degree of accuracy including decimal places and significant figures. Round answers to a reasonable degree of accuracy in the context of a given problem.
sections.extended_content: Give upper and lower bounds for data given to a specified accuracy.

sections.title: Standard form
sections.core_content: Understand and use standard form to express very large and very small numbers in the form A × 10^n where 1 ≤ A < 10 and n is an integer. Convert numbers to and from standard form. Calculate with values in standard form.

sections.title: Four operations
sections.core_content: Use the four operations for calculations with integers, fractions and decimals, including correct ordering of operations and use of brackets.

sections.title: Estimation
sections.core_content: Make estimates of numbers, quantities and lengths. Estimate the results of calculations. Round answers to a reasonable degree of accuracy in the context of a given problem.

sections.title: Limits of accuracy
sections.extended_content: Give upper and lower bounds for data given to a specified accuracy. Find upper and lower bounds of the results of calculations involving quantities given to specified accuracies.

sections.title: Ratio and proportion
sections.core_content: Use ratio notation, including reduction to simplest form. Divide a quantity in a given ratio. Use proportion to solve problems. Calculate average speed, distance, time. Use common measures of rate.
sections.extended_content: Increase and decrease a quantity by a given ratio.

sections.title: Percentages
sections.core_content: Calculate a given percentage of a quantity. Express one quantity as a percentage of another. Calculate percentage increase or decrease. Calculate simple and compound interest.
sections.extended_content: Carry out reverse percentage calculations. Calculate repeated percentage change.

sections.title: Using a calculator
sections.core_content: Use a scientific calculator efficiently. Apply appropriate checks of accuracy. Interpret the calculator display appropriately.

sections.title: Time, speed and rates
sections.core_content: Calculate times in terms of the 24-hour and 12-hour clock. Read clocks, dials and timetables. Calculate average speed, distance and time. Use common measures of rate including cost per unit.
sections.extended_content: Use compound measures such as speed, density, pressure and population density in solving problems.

sections.title: Money and finance
sections.core_content: Solve problems involving money and convert from one currency to another. Use given data to solve problems on personal and household finance involving earnings, simple and compound interest, discount, profit and loss.

sections.title: Exponential growth and decay
sections.extended_content: Recognise and use exponential growth and decay. Set up and solve growth and decay problems.

sections.title: Surds
sections.extended_content: Understand and use surds, including simplifying expressions. Rationalise the denominator of a fraction.

sections.title: Algebraic notation and substitution
sections.core_content: Use letters to express generalised numbers and express basic arithmetic processes algebraically. Substitute numbers for words and letters in formulae and expressions. Understand and use the concepts of expression, equation, formula, identity, term, variable, constant, coefficient.
sections.extended_content: Construct and transform more complex formulae and expressions.

sections.title: Rearranging formulae
sections.core_content: Rearrange simple formulae. Change the subject of a formula.
sections.extended_content: Rearrange formulae where the subject appears in more than one term, or where a power of the subject appears.

sections.title: Algebraic manipulation
sections.core_content: Collect like terms. Expand products of algebraic expressions. Factorise by extracting common factors. Factorise expressions of the form ax + bx + kay + kby. Expand the product of two linear expressions.
sections.extended_content: Factorise expressions of the form a²x² − b²y² (difference of squares). Complete the square for expressions in the form x² + bx + c. Factorise quadratic expressions of the form ax² + bx + c.

sections.title: Algebraic fractions
sections.extended_content: Manipulate algebraic fractions: add, subtract, multiply and divide algebraic fractions. Simplify algebraic fractions. Factorise and simplify rational expressions.

sections.title: Equations
sections.core_content: Solve linear equations in one unknown. Solve simultaneous linear equations in two unknowns algebraically and graphically.
sections.extended_content: Solve quadratic equations by factorising, using the quadratic formula and completing the square. Solve simultaneous equations where one is linear and one is non-linear.

sections.title: Inequalities
sections.core_content: Represent and interpret inequalities on a number line. Solve simple linear inequalities.
sections.extended_content: Solve quadratic inequalities in one unknown and represent the solution set on a number line. Represent inequalities graphically.

sections.title: Sequences
sections.core_content: Continue a given number sequence. Recognise patterns in sequences including the term-to-term rule and the position-to-term rule. Find the nth term of a linear sequence.
sections.extended_content: Find the nth term of simple quadratic sequences. Recognise and use sequences of triangular, square, cube numbers, Fibonacci type and simple geometric progressions.

sections.title: Direct and inverse proportion
sections.extended_content: Express direct and inverse proportion in algebraic terms and use this form of expression to find unknown quantities. Set up and use equations to solve problems involving direct and inverse proportion.

sections.title: Graphs in practical situations
sections.core_content: Interpret and use graphs in practical situations including travel graphs and conversion graphs. Draw graphs from given data. Apply the idea of rate of change to simple kinematics involving distance–time and speed–time graphs.
sections.extended_content: Calculate distance travelled as area under a speed–time graph. Interpret the gradient of a tangent at a point on a curve as the instantaneous rate of change.

sections.title: Graphs of functions
sections.core_content: Construct tables of values and draw graphs of functions of the form y = ax^n where n = −2, −1, 0, 1, 2, 3. Interpret graphs of linear, quadratic, cubic and reciprocal functions. Solve associated equations approximately by graphical methods.
sections.extended_content: Draw and interpret graphs of exponential functions y = a^x. Estimate gradients of curves by drawing tangents. Solve equations approximately using graphical methods.

sections.title: Sketching curves
sections.extended_content: Recognise, sketch and interpret graphs of: linear, quadratic, cubic, reciprocal, exponential and trigonometric functions. Know the shapes of these graphs and use them to sketch curves.

sections.title: Differentiation
sections.extended_content: Estimate gradients of curves by drawing tangents. Differentiate integer powers of x. Determine gradients, rates of change, turning points (maxima and minima) and stationary points. Apply differentiation to gradients, tangents and normals, and stationary points.

sections.title: Functions
sections.extended_content: Understand function notation, e.g. f(x) = 3x − 5, f : x ↦ 3x − 5. Understand and find inverse functions f⁻¹(x). Form composite functions fg(x). Use the relationships between a function and its inverse.

sections.title: Coordinates
sections.core_content: Use and interpret Cartesian coordinates in two dimensions. Plot points and read coordinates from graphs.

sections.title: Drawing linear graphs
sections.core_content: Draw straight-line graphs from their equations using a table of values. Recognise that equations of the form y = mx + c correspond to straight-line graphs.

sections.title: Gradient of linear graphs
sections.core_content: Find the gradient of a straight line from coordinates of two points on it. Calculate the gradient of a straight line from its equation. Understand that parallel lines have the same gradient.

sections.title: Length and midpoint
sections.extended_content: Calculate the length of a line segment from the coordinates of its end points. Find the midpoint of a line segment from the coordinates of its end points.
questionTopics: Length And Midpoint Of A Line Segment

sections.title: Equations of linear graphs
sections.core_content: Interpret and obtain the equation of a straight-line graph in the form y = mx + c. Determine the equation of a straight line parallel to a given line.
sections.extended_content: Find the equation of a straight line given the gradient and one point, or given two points.

sections.title: Parallel lines
sections.core_content: Find the gradient and equation of a straight line parallel to a given line.

sections.title: Perpendicular lines
sections.extended_content: Find the gradient and equation of a straight line perpendicular to a given line. Know that perpendicular lines have gradients whose product is −1.

sections.title: Geometrical terms
sections.core_content: Use and interpret the geometrical terms: point, line, plane, parallel, perpendicular, bearing, right angle, acute, obtuse and reflex angles. Use and interpret vocabulary of triangles, special quadrilaterals, polygons and simple solid figures including nets.

sections.title: Geometrical constructions
sections.core_content: Measure and draw lines and angles. Construct a triangle given the three sides using a ruler and compasses only. Construct other simple geometrical figures from given data using ruler, protractor and compasses. Construct angle bisectors and perpendicular bisectors using compasses.
sections.extended_content: Construct loci (sets of points equidistant from a point, a line, or satisfying other given criteria).

sections.title: Scale drawings and bearings
sections.core_content: Draw and interpret scale drawings. Use and interpret three-figure bearings measured clockwise from the North. Understand and use the relationship between scales and distances.

sections.title: Similarity and congruence
sections.core_content: Recognise congruent shapes and similar shapes. Calculate lengths of similar figures. Use the relationships between areas and volumes of similar figures.
sections.extended_content: Use the relationships between areas of similar triangles, and between volumes and surface areas of similar solids.

sections.title: Symmetry
sections.core_content: Recognise line symmetry and rotational symmetry in two dimensions. Identify lines of symmetry and order of rotational symmetry.
sections.extended_content: Recognise symmetry properties of prisms, cylinders, cones and pyramids.

sections.title: Angles
sections.core_content: Calculate unknown angles using the following geometrical properties: angles at a point, angles at a point on a straight line, vertically opposite angles. Angles formed within parallel lines (corresponding, alternate, co-interior). Angle properties of triangles and quadrilaterals. Angle sum of a polygon. Interior and exterior angles of a polygon.

sections.title: Circle theorems
sections.extended_content: Calculate unknown angles using circle theorems: angle in a semicircle = 90°, angle at centre = 2× angle at circumference, angles in same segment are equal, opposite angles of cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180°, tangent perpendicular to radius, two tangents from an external point are equal, alternate segment theorem.

sections.title: Constructions and loci
sections.extended_content: Use the following loci and the method of intersecting loci for sets of points in two dimensions: distance from a point, distance from a straight line, equidistant from two points, equidistant from two straight lines.

sections.title: Units of measure
sections.core_content: Use current units of mass, length, area, volume and capacity in practical situations. Express quantities in terms of larger or smaller units. Convert between units including units of area and volume.

sections.title: Area and perimeter
sections.core_content: Carry out calculations involving the perimeter and area of a rectangle, triangle, parallelogram and trapezium. Find the area of compound shapes.

sections.title: Circles, arcs and sectors
sections.core_content: Carry out calculations involving the circumference and area of a circle. Solve problems involving arc length and sector area as fractions of the circumference and area of a circle.

sections.title: Surface area and volume
sections.core_content: Carry out calculations involving the surface area and volume of a cuboid, prism, cylinder, sphere, pyramid and cone.
sections.extended_content: Carry out calculations involving the surface area and volume of a sphere, pyramid and cone. Find the surface area and volume of compound solids.

sections.title: Compound shapes
sections.core_content: Carry out calculations involving compound shapes made from two or more rectangles, triangles, circles or parts of circles.
sections.extended_content: Carry out calculations involving the frustum of a cone or pyramid. Find the volume and surface area of compound solids.

sections.title: Pythagoras' theorem
sections.core_content: Know and use Pythagoras' theorem to calculate the length of a side of a right-angled triangle.

sections.title: Right-angled triangles
sections.core_content: Know and use the sine, cosine and tangent ratios for acute angles in calculations involving sides and angles of a right-angled triangle. Solve problems in two dimensions using Pythagoras' theorem and trigonometry.
sections.extended_content: Solve trigonometric problems in two dimensions including those involving angles of elevation and depression.

sections.title: Exact trigonometric values
sections.extended_content: Know the exact values of sin, cos and tan for 0°, 30°, 45°, 60° and 90°. Use these exact values in calculations.

sections.title: Trigonometric functions
sections.extended_content: Recognise, sketch and interpret graphs of y = sin x, y = cos x, y = tan x for 0° ≤ x ≤ 360°. Solve simple trigonometric equations for values in this range.

sections.title: Non-right-angled triangles
sections.extended_content: Use the sine rule and the cosine rule to solve problems involving triangles. Use the formula Area = ½ab sin C for the area of a triangle.

sections.title: 3D trigonometry
sections.extended_content: Solve problems in three dimensions using Pythagoras' theorem and trigonometry, including finding the angle between a line and a plane.

sections.title: Transformations
sections.core_content: Describe and perform reflections, rotations, translations and enlargements. Recognise and describe transformations. Describe combined transformations.
sections.extended_content: Use the following transformations: reflection (including y = x, y = −x), rotation, translation, enlargement (including fractional and negative scale factors). Describe fully a single transformation that is equivalent to a combination of transformations.

sections.title: Vectors in 2D
sections.core_content: Describe a translation by using a vector. Add and subtract vectors. Multiply a vector by a scalar.

sections.title: Magnitude of a vector
sections.extended_content: Calculate the magnitude of a vector. Use unit vectors and position vectors.

sections.title: Vector geometry
sections.extended_content: Use vectors to solve geometric problems. Apply vector methods to establish simple proofs. Use vectors to find midpoints, to show collinearity, and to solve other geometric problems.

sections.title: Introduction to probability
sections.core_content: Understand and use the probability scale from 0 to 1. Find and justify probabilities based on equally likely outcomes. Understand that the probability of an event occurring = 1 − the probability of the event not occurring.

sections.title: Relative and expected frequencies
sections.core_content: Understand relative frequency as an estimate of probability. Calculate expected frequencies.

sections.title: Combined events
sections.core_content: Calculate the probability of combined events using tree diagrams, Venn diagrams, sample space diagrams, or other diagrammatic representations. Apply the addition and multiplication rules.
sections.extended_content: Understand when to add and when to multiply probabilities. Apply these rules to more complex problems.

sections.title: Conditional probability
sections.extended_content: Calculate conditional probabilities using tree diagrams, Venn diagrams, two-way tables and other methods.

sections.title: Classifying and tabulating data
sections.core_content: Classify and tabulate statistical data. Read, interpret and draw simple inferences from tables and statistical diagrams. Understand the difference between discrete and continuous data.

sections.title: Interpreting statistical data
sections.core_content: Read and interpret information from tables, charts and graphs. Draw simple inferences from data. Compare sets of data using tables, graphs and statistical measures.

sections.title: Averages and measures of spread
sections.core_content: Calculate the mean, median, mode and range for individual and discrete frequency distributions. Calculate an estimate of the mean for grouped and continuous data. Identify the modal class from grouped data.
sections.extended_content: Calculate quartiles and the interquartile range. Construct and interpret box-and-whisker plots.

sections.title: Statistical charts and diagrams
sections.core_content: Draw and interpret bar charts, pie charts, pictograms, stem-and-leaf diagrams, and simple frequency distributions. Construct and use frequency tables, bar charts, pie charts.

sections.title: Scatter diagrams and correlation
sections.core_content: Draw and interpret scatter diagrams. Understand positive, negative and zero correlation. Draw a line of best fit by eye and use it to make predictions.

sections.title: Cumulative frequency
sections.extended_content: Construct and interpret cumulative frequency tables and diagrams. Estimate and interpret the median, quartiles, percentiles and interquartile range from cumulative frequency diagrams.

sections.title: Histograms
sections.extended_content: Construct and interpret histograms with equal and unequal class intervals. Calculate frequency density.
